title: Ochna verruculosa
intro: Ochna verruculosa, also known as the scurf-leaf ochna, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ochna verruculosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiproliferative activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Ochna verruculosa is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and sore throats.
